_comment: Policy for OllamaCapabilityPicker.bestInstalledModel(for:mode:). Maps each TaskCategory to required/preferred Ollama capability tags (per harness/ollama-catalog.json), and dictates how the size-tier dial behaves under each routing mode. Read at picker-call time. Edit + git push + (relaunch OR refreshMchataisourceCache) ships changes with no Swift rebuild. Built-in fallback in OllamaCapabilityPicker.swift mirrors this file for offline first-launch.

categories._comment: required = hard filter (model MUST have all these caps to be considered). preferred = soft signal — more matches gets bonus rank. Cap names match the OllamaCatalogModel.Capability enum: chat, code, vision, reasoning, multilingual, embedding, tools.

sizePolicy._comment: Size dial per (mode, category). Three behaviors: (1) `simpleSmallestFirst`: for .simple, pick the smallest chat-capable installed model — fast intent classification doesn't need a big model. (2) `lowestCostMinSizeGB`: in Lowest Cost mode, when category isn't quality-critical, gate models below this size out (avoids picking a tiny chat model that produces poor output). (3) `qualityCriticalCategories`: even in Lowest Cost, these categories prefer bigger-is-better — capability matters more than the small cost-saving from a smaller model.

simpleNonVisionPreference._comment: For .simple (intent classification, routing decisions) prefer text-focused models. Vision-tuned models load a big image encoder that's wasted weight on text-only inputs. When the user has BOTH vision and non-vision chat-capable models installed (e.g. {qwen3-vl:8b, gpt-oss:20b}), pick the non-vision one. Falls back to vision if it's the only chat-capable option.
